proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide (univariable, unadjusted).
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone (univariable, unadjusted).
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel (univariable, unadjusted).
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib (univariable, unadjusted).
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma (univariable, unadjusted).
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab (univariable, unadjusted).

analyses.code: # two-proportion z-test: objective_response on vs off treatment_enzalutamide

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for all features and the other treatments, treatment_enzalutamide remains positively associated with objective_response.
After adjustment, treatment_abiraterone is associated with objective_response.
After adjustment, treatment_docetaxel is associated with objective_response.
After adjustment, treatment_olaparib is associated with objective_response.
After adjustment, treatment_lu177_psma is associated with objective_response.
After adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with objective_response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with mcrpc=0.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with visceral_mets=0.
Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than brca2-wildtype patients.
Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than ar_v7_positive=0 patients.
Patients with msi_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than msi_high=0 patients.
Patients with psma_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than psma_high=0 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
age_years is associated with objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is smaller in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than in ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).
The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is smaller in brca2_mutation=1 patients than in brca2_mutation=0 patients (negative interaction).
The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is smaller in msi_high=1 patients than in msi_high=0 patients (negative interaction).
treatment_olaparib has a larger positive effect on objective_response in brca2_mutation=1 patients than in brca2_mutation=0 patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab has a larger positive effect on objective_response in msi_high=1 patients than in msi_high=0 patients.
treatment_lu177_psma has a larger positive effect on objective_response in psma_high=1 patients than in psma_high=0 patients.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ treatment_enzalutamide + ar_v7_positive + treatment_enzalutamide*ar_v7_positive + other treatments + key prognostics).fit() — read interaction term

Logit(y ~ treatment_enzalutamide + msi_high + treatment_enzalutamide*msi_high + other treatments + key prognostics).fit() — read interaction term
Logit(y ~ treatment_olaparib + brca2_mutation + treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation + other treatments + key prognostics).fit() — read interaction term
Logit(y ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + msi_high + treatment_pembrolizumab*msi_high + other treatments + key prognostics).fit() — read interaction term
Logit(y ~ treatment_lu177_psma + psma_high + treatment_lu177_psma*psma_high + other treatments + key prognostics).fit() — read interaction term

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response rate.
Within ar_v7_positive=1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide does not raise objective_response rate.
Within mcrpc=0 patients, treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response rate.
Within mcrpc=1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide does not meaningfully raise objective_response rate.
Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide does not raise objective_response rate.
Within msi_high=1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide does not raise objective_response rate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients with psa_ng_ml below the cohort median (~15.86), treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response rate by a larger margin than in patients with high PSA.
In patients with psa_ng_ml above the cohort median, treatment_enzalutamide still raises objective_response rate but by a smaller margin.

In patients with mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than control.
In patients with mcrpc=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than control.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients with mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0 (the joint biomarker-favorable subgroup), treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response rate from approximately 17% to approximately 80%.

In patients who fall outside this 4-way favorable subgroup (i.e., have mcrpc=1 OR ar_v7_positive=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 OR msi_high=1), treatment_enzalutamide produces no clinically meaningful improvement in objective_response.

z-test on the complement subgroup (any unfavorable biomarker)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Restricting attention to mcrpc=1 patients (relaxing only the mcrpc favorable criterion) is sufficient to abolish the enzalutamide benefit.
Restricting attention to ar_v7_positive=1 patients is sufficient to abolish the enzalutamide benefit.
Restricting attention to brca2_mutation=1 patients is sufficient to abolish the enzalutamide benefit.
Restricting attention to msi_high=1 patients is sufficient to abolish the enzalutamide benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Best docetaxel subgroup (brca2_mutation=1 & msi_high=1): rate_on=0.125 (n=48) vs rate_off=0.234 (n=94); diff=-0.1090; p=0.1224. No docetaxel subgroup achieved clinically meaningful significant effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model containing all 6 treatments + the 5 plausible matched biomarker-treatment interactions, the enzalutamide x ar_v7_positive interaction is significantly negative.
In the same model, the olaparib x brca2_mutation interaction is not significantly positive (and may be slightly negative).
In the same model, the pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction is not significant.
In the same model, the lu177_psma x psma_high interaction is not significant.

In the same model, the ar_v7_positive interaction with enzalutamide remains strongly negative.

In the same model, the msi_high interaction with enzalutamide remains strongly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the 4-way favorable subgroup additionally restricted to psa_ng_ml ≤ 15.86, treatment_enzalutamide produces the largest absolute increase in objective_response observed in this cohort.

analyses.code: z-test of enzalutamide effect within mcrpc=0 & ar_v7=0 & brca2=0 & msi=0 & psa_ng_ml<=15.86
analyses.result_summary: In favorable + low-PSA subgroup: rate_on=0.809 (n=4938) vs rate_off=0.179 (n=7266); diff=+0.6299; p=0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The final best-supported treatment-effect subgroup for objective_response is: in patients with mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide raises the objective_response rate from approximately 17% to approximately 80% (a positive effect, ~63 percentage point absolute increase).
treatment_olaparib does not raise objective_response in any subgroup tested, including the canonical brca2_mutation=1 target subgroup, where its effect is null to slightly negative.
treatment_pembrolizumab does not raise objective_response in any subgroup tested, including the canonical msi_high=1 target subgroup.
treatment_lu177_psma does not raise objective_response in any subgroup tested, including the canonical psma_high=1 target subgroup.
treatment_abiraterone has no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response.
treatment_docetaxel has no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariable abiraterone diff=-0.0031 (p=0.4628); adjusted coef=-0.0250 (OR=0.975, p=0.3107).

analyses.code: Reaffirms iter1+iter2 main and adjusted docetaxel effect.
analyses.result_summary: Univariable docetaxel diff=-0.0005 (p=0.9054); adjusted coef=+0.0016 (OR=1.002, p=0.947).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After full adjustment, higher psa_ng_ml remains negatively associated with objective_response.
After full adjustment, higher ecog_ps remains negatively associated with objective_response.
After full adjustment, higher albumin_g_dl remains positively associated with objective_response.
After full adjustment, higher weight_loss_pct_6mo remains negatively associated with objective_response.
